page.title: Immaculate Conception Yacht Charter in Marbella: A Festive Day Afloat

data.meta_description: Celebrate Immaculate Conception with a yacht charter in Marbella. Sail from Puerto Banús with skipper, drinks & snacks included from €749/2h. Book now.
data.summary: A guide to chartering a yacht in Marbella for the Immaculate Conception holiday, with fleet details, pricing, and local cruising tips from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Immaculate Conception (8 December) is a public holiday in Spain, ideal for a day afloat.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella, with a licensed skipper included.
Prices start at €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39), including drinks and snacks.
Mangusta 80 (24m) charters from €4,719 for 4 hours, with a free jet ski.
Other boats: pricing on request via WhatsApp – no hidden fees.
Every charter includes fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), and a full drinks menu.
Popular destinations: Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Cala del Faro.
Book early – Immaculate Conception is a peak day for Marbella yacht charters.

faq.q: What is the Immaculate Conception holiday in Spain?
faq.a: Immaculate Conception (8 December) is a national public holiday in Spain, marking a Catholic feast day. Many locals and tourists take the day off, making it a popular time for leisure activities like yacht charters in Marbella.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht for a half-day on Immaculate Conception?
faq.a: Yes. Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39) are available from 2 hours upwards, with 4-hour and 6-hour options. The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking. For other boats, contact us via WhatsApp for flexible durations.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No extra charges – just show up.

faq.q: Where can we sail to from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Popular routes include Cabopino (20 min east), Estepona (30 min west), or further to Sotogrande (45 min). You can also anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for swimming. Your skipper will tailor the itinerary.

faq.q: How many people can we bring on a charter?
faq.a: Capacity varies: Astondoa 40 (9 pax), Azimut 39 (11 pax), Mangusta 80 (12 pax), and smaller boats like the Speedboat (6 pax). Check our fleet page for exact limits.

faq.q: Is the Immaculate Conception a busy day for yacht charters?
faq.a: Yes – December 8th is a popular holiday in Andalusia. Many families and groups book ahead. We recommend reserving at least two weeks in advance to secure your preferred boat and time slot.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and decorations?
faq.a: Absolutely. You are welcome to bring your own catering, birthday cakes, or festive decorations. Our crew will help with setup. Just let us know in advance.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on Immaculate Conception?
faq.a: The skipper will assess conditions on the day. If it is unsafe to sail, we offer a full refund or reschedule to another date. Marbella enjoys mild December weather, often 16–20°C.

data.body_html:
Immaculate Conception (8 December) is a public holiday in Spain – a perfect excuse to trade the office for the open sea. With a Marbella yacht charter, you can celebrate this festive day afloat, cruising the Costa del Sol in style. Departing from Puerto Banús, our fleet offers everything from sleek speedboats to superyachts, with all the trimmings included. Whether you want a quick 2-hour spin or an all-day escape, here’s everything you need to plan your Immaculate Conception yacht charter in Marbella.

Why charter a yacht on Immaculate Conception?

Immaculate Conception is a national holiday in Spain, meaning locals have the day off and the coastline comes alive. Instead of crowded beaches or busy marinas, you can enjoy the calm of the Mediterranean with your own private yacht. December temperatures in Marbella hover around 16–20°C, with plenty of sunshine – ideal for a day on the water. Plus, chartering on a weekday holiday often means quieter seas and more availability at anchorages like Cabopino or Cala del Faro.

Our fleet for Immaculate Conception charters

We operate from Puerto Banús with a diverse fleet. For a small group, the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) or Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) are great for a quick outing. Families and friends often choose the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – both starting at €749 for 2 hours. For a luxury experience, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) costs from €4,719 for 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. Other boats like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86 are available on request via WhatsApp.

Pricing and inclusions for Immaculate Conception

All prices include a licensed skipper, fuel, and a full drinks menu (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava) plus light snacks. Insurance and Spanish IVA (21%) are also covered – no hidden fees. Here’s a quick pricing table for our most popular options:

BoatLengthMax PaxPrice (2h)Price (8h)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749€2,299
Azimut 3912.5m11€749€2,299
Mangusta 8024m12€4,719 (4h min)On request

For other boats, pricing is on request – just drop us a WhatsApp message.

Best routes and anchorages in December

From Puerto Banús, head east to Cabopino (20 minutes) – a quiet cove with soft sand and calm waters. Westwards, Estepona (30 minutes) offers a pretty marina and beachfront. For a longer cruise, Sotogrande (45 minutes) is a luxury enclave. On the way, stop at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for a swim (the water is still swimmable in December – around 16°C). Your skipper knows the best spots and can adjust the route to your preferences.

What to bring and wear

Pack layers – a light jacket or sweater is useful for the breeze, even in the sun. Sunglasses, sunscreen (SPF 30+), and a hat are essential. You can bring your own food, decorations, or a cake to celebrate. The boat has a fridge and icebox for your provisions. Don’t forget a camera – the views of La Concha mountain from the sea are stunning in winter light.

How to book your Immaculate Conception yacht charter

Reservations are easy. Browse our full fleet on the Boat Rental Marbella homepage, then contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred boat, date, and duration. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance for Immaculate Conception, as availability is limited. Payment is secure, and we confirm your booking within 24 hours. For last-minute inquiries, call or message us – we often have same-day openings.

Why choose Boat Rental Marbella?

We are a local operator based in Puerto Banús, not an affiliate. Every boat on our fleet is ours to charter, so you deal directly with us. Our skippers are experienced, English and Spanish speaking, and know the coast intimately. We include everything in the price – no surprise extras. Check our Immaculate Conception yacht charter Marbella page for more tips, and read our blog for seasonal guides.

Frequently asked questions

What is the Immaculate Conception holiday in Spain?

Immaculate Conception (8 December) is a national public holiday in Spain, marking a Catholic feast day. Many locals and tourists take the day off, making it a popular time for leisure activities like yacht charters in Marbella.

Can I charter a yacht for a half-day on Immaculate Conception?

Yes. Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39) are available from 2 hours upwards, with 4-hour and 6-hour options. The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking. For other boats, contact us via WhatsApp for flexible durations.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No extra charges – just show up.

Where can we sail to from Puerto Banús?

Popular routes include Cabopino (20 min east), Estepona (30 min west), or further to Sotogrande (45 min). You can also anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for swimming. Your skipper will tailor the itinerary.

How many people can we bring on a charter?

Capacity varies: Astondoa 40 (9 pax), Azimut 39 (11 pax), Mangusta 80 (12 pax), and smaller boats like the Speedboat (6 pax). Check our fleet page for exact limits.

Is the Immaculate Conception a busy day for yacht charters?

Yes – December 8th is a popular holiday in Andalusia. Many families and groups book ahead. We recommend reserving at least two weeks in advance to secure your preferred boat and time slot.

Can we bring our own food and decorations?

Absolutely. You are welcome to bring your own catering, birthday cakes, or festive decorations. Our crew will help with setup. Just let us know in advance.

What happens if the weather is bad on Immaculate Conception?

The skipper will assess conditions on the day. If it is unsafe to sail, we offer a full refund or reschedule to another date. Marbella enjoys mild December weather, often 16–20°C.
